content_snippet:
@echo off
set PASSWORD=Sup3rS3cret2024!
set DB_HOST=dbprod.corp.local
mysql -uroot -p%PASSWORD% -h %DB_HOST% -e "SELECT NOW()"
notes: Windows batch file uses CMD 'set PASSWORD=value' to stage a credential in env. Snaffler PR #198 adds this pattern — operator-validated. Red.

content_snippet:
@echo off
set "PGPASSWORD=W3lc0m3!"
set "PGUSER=postgres"
pg_dump -h db.corp.local -U postgres mydb > backup.sql
notes: Quoted-string variant of CMD set — 'set "VAR=value"'. Per PR #198. Red.

content_snippet:
# Bootstrap Azure resources
az login --username admin@corp.onmicrosoft.com --password 'P@ssw0rd2024!'
az account set --subscription production
az vm list --output table
notes: Azure CLI 'az login --password' embeds tenant-admin creds directly in script. Per PR #155 — operator filed bug that Snaffler misses Azure auth scripts. Red.
Azure CLI service-principal login with embedded client secret. Same #155 PR scope. Red.
XML <password> containing nested tag (value, hash, b64 etc). Snaffler's PR #124 noted their existing regex assumed <password>X< would always be FP. Real pattern in legacy enterprise apps. Red.
'credential' (singular/plural) keyword loosely in filename. Per PR #98 — operator added 'credential' to KeepFilenameContainsPamOrPwdVault after seeing it across multiple engagements. Red.
Camel-case 'Credentials' in filename. Same loose-keyword scope as PR #98. Red.

Chrome saved-passwords SQLite DB. The Firefox-rule cousin — same operator complaint scope (#46 'browser stored password databases'), different vendor. Tests whether v0.47's Firefox-shaped rule generalizes to other browsers. Black.
Edge saved-passwords (same Chromium-base as Chrome). Same browser-creds meta-pattern. Black.
CMD 'set CLIENT_SECRET=...' — OAuth/service-principal client secret in batch. Adjacent to #198 but distinct keyword. Red.
